commands.review_summary:
The PR moves local memory embeddings behind a forked worker by default, adds structured worker-failure handling, degrades to configured fallback or FTS-only search after local worker failure, and adds focused memory/config/build-entry coverage.

Reproducibility: Partially: the linked report at https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/44202 has concrete Apple Silicon ggml-metal abort logs, and current main still constructs local node-llama embeddings in the gateway process. I did not reproduce the native crash live in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Protected maintainer draft with an upgrade-sensitive default process boundary; the next step is maintainer review plus packaged/live proof, not an automated repair PR.

target.title: Fix local embedding worker safety
target.body:
## Summary

- Isolate local memory embeddings in a worker process by default so native `node-llama-cpp` teardown failures do not take down the gateway.
- Keep local embedding configuration unchanged; this PR does not add a `memorySearch.local.gpu` key or a QMD CPU policy knob.
- Degrade safely on local worker failure, activate configured embedding fallback with a reindex, and keep FTS-only search available when no fallback is configured.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/embeddings.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-embedding-timeout.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/search-manager.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.mistral-provider.test.ts src/config/config.schema-regressions.test.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts src/agents/memory-search.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-44202-local-embedding-safety.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-44202-local-embedding-safety.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.plugin-sdk.dts.json --declaration true --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/plugin-sdk-dts-44202-local-embedding-safety.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/tsdown-build.mjs`
- `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/reference/memory-config.md`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Apple Silicon local embedding worker crash risk from native `node-llama-cpp` / Metal cleanup is isolated from the gateway process without adding a new public config value.
Real environment tested: macOS local worktree, Node 24.16.0, focused unit/type/build proof.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: see Verification above; also checked built `dist/memory-core-local-embedding-worker.js` and the bundled embeddings chunk resolve the worker sidecar, checked generated bundles contain no `gpuLayers`, `QMD_FORCE_CPU`, or local GPU policy names, and launched the built default sidecar path far enough to receive the expected missing-optional-dependency error from inside the worker in this checkout.
Evidence after fix: focused tests passed, typechecks passed, build passed, docs MDX passed, artifact check confirmed the worker sidecar exists and close ack is not followed by child `process.disconnect`.
Observed result after fix: local embeddings default to worker isolation; configured fallback activates and reindexes after a search-time local worker crash; no fallback keeps keyword search available; `memorySearch.local.gpu` is rejected by config validation.
What was not tested: a live Apple Silicon Metal teardown crash, because the local repro path did not reproduce the native crash; full local embedding startup with real `node-llama-cpp`, because this checkout does not have that optional runtime package installed.

Fixes #44202
target.author: osolmaz
